perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees to protect the Information of the Disclosing Party against unauthorized disclosure and warrants that it applies reasonable safeguards against the unauthorized disclosure Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will endeavor to protect the Information of the Disclosing Party against unauthorized disclosure and warrants that it will attempt to apply reasonable safeguards against unauthorized disclosure of Information.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces a contradiction by changing 'agrees to protect' to 'will endeavor to protect,' weakening the obligation. Originally, the Receiving Party had a firm commitment to protect information. Now, they only 'endeavor' to do so. This creates ambiguity and contradicts Section 5 which states 'The Information shall be treated as confidential and safeguarded hereunder by the Receiving Party,' because 'endeavor' and 'attempt' imply less stringent safeguarding, creating uncertainty about whether the original confidential treatment is guaranteed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Information shall be treated as confidential and safeguarded hereunder by the Receiving Party for a period of two (2) years.
perturbation.justification: The original text imposed a firm obligation on the Receiving Party to 'protect' the Disclosing Party's information. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'endeavor to protect'. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that 'The Information shall be treated as confidential and safeguarded hereunder by the Receiving Party,' because 'endeavor' implies a weaker commitment than 'safeguarded', creating uncertainty about the level of protection and thus a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that: (i) the Information shall be used solely for the purpose described in the preamble to this Agreement; (ii) it will not use any Information disclosed hereunder for any other purpose; and (iii) it will not distribute, disclosure or disseminate Information to anyone except its employees and agents with a need to know and who, in 1 <PAGE> 2 each case, have been informed of the confidential nature of the Information and have agreed to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that: (i) the Information may be used for purposes related to the general business interests of the Receiving Party; (ii) it will use Information disclosed hereunder as it sees fit; and (iii) it will distribute, disclose or disseminate Information to its employees and agents. The Receiving party is encouraged to inform such employees and agents of the confidential nature of the Information but is not required to do so.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text contradicts the original confidentiality obligations. The Receiving Party can now use the information for general business interests, use it 'as it sees fit,' and distribute it to employees/agents without ensuring they're informed of confidentiality. This directly contradicts Section 3, stating that 'Information of the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party,' because the Receiving Party now has broad latitude to use the information without regard to the Disclosing Party's proprietary rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Information of the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party agrees to protect the Information of the Disclosing Party against unauthorized disclosure and warrants that it applies reasonable safeguards against the unauthorized disclosure Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and contradicted text emphasizes confidentiality and protection of the disclosing party's information. However, the changed text allows the Receiving Party to use the information for its general business interests, use it 'as it sees fit,' and distribute it to employees/agents without ensuring they're informed of confidentiality. This directly violates the contradicted text stating 'Information of the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party' and the Receiving Party's obligation to protect it.

perturbation.original_text: The Information shall be treated as confidential and safeguarded hereunder by the Receiving Party for a period of two (2) years.
perturbation.changed_text: The Information shall be treated as confidential and safeguarded hereunder by the Receiving Party for a period of one (1) year.
perturbation.explanation: The modification directly contradicts the agreement's initial intent by reducing the confidentiality period from two years to one year. The Receiving Party is no longer obligated to safeguard the Disclosing Party's confidential information for the duration initially agreed upon. This weakens the entire confidentiality framework established by the agreement, because it is contradicting Section 7, by allowing the Receiving Party to solicit employees of the Disclosing party sooner
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the above-mentioned business combination is not completed, each Party agrees not to solicit, entice or offer employment to any employees of the other Party before one (1) year from the date of this Letter; provided, however, that the foregoing shall not prohibit either Party from employing any individual who has received notice of termination from, or ceased to be employed by, the other Party prior to the first time such individuals discussed, directly or with any representatives, employment by the hiring Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the confidentiality period from two years to one year. The contradicted text states that neither party will solicit employees of the other party for one year from the date of the letter. The explanation states that by reducing the confidentiality from two years to one, this allows the Receiving Party to solicit employees of the Disclosing party sooner, which creates a direct contradiction of not soliciting until a year has passed.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party acknowledges that in its examination of the Information it will be exposed to material nonpublic information concerning the business and financial condition of the Disclosing Party and consequently the Receiving Party agrees that prior to the date two (2) years from the date hereof, without the prior written approval of the Board of Directors of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will not (and will insure that its affiliates (and any person acting on behalf of or in concern with the Receiving Party or any affiliate) will not) purchase or otherwise acquire (or enter into any agreement or 2 <PAGE> 3 make any proposal to purchase or otherwise acquire) any securities of the Disclosing Party, any warrant or option to purchase such securities, any security convertible into any such securities or any other right to acquire such securities.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party acknowledges that in its examination of the Information it may be exposed to material nonpublic information concerning the business and financial condition of the Disclosing Party and consequently the Receiving Party agrees that prior to the date one (1) year from the date hereof, the Receiving Party will attempt to avoid purchasing or otherwise acquiring (or entering into any agreement or 2 <PAGE> 3 make any proposal to purchase or otherwise acquire) any securities of the Disclosing Party, any warrant or option to purchase such securities, any security convertible into any such securities or any other right to acquire such securities.
perturbation.explanation: The change weakens the restriction on purchasing securities by the Receiving Party. 'May be exposed' is less definite than 'will be exposed', and 'attempt to avoid purchasing' is weaker than 'will not purchase'. Also, the restricted period is reduced to one year which contradicts the two-year period stated in section 5. This now introduces uncertainty over whether the Receiving party will safeguard the Disclosing Party's financial information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Information shall be treated as confidential and safeguarded hereunder by the Receiving Party for a period of two (2) years.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the restricted period to one year, directly contradicting the 'two (2) years' mentioned in the contradicted text. Also, the change from 'will be exposed' to 'may be exposed' and 'will not purchase' to 'attempt to avoid purchasing' weakens the original commitment, creating uncertainty about safeguarding the Disclosing Party's financial information for the specified two-year period. This inconsistency in the duration of confidentiality and the level of commitment to avoiding purchasing securities directly violates the original terms, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: At the Disclosing Party's request, all Information of the Disclosing Party in tangible form, or any copies thereof, that is in the possession of the Receiving Party shall be returned to the Disclosing Party or destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: At the Disclosing Party's request, all Information of the Disclosing Party in tangible form, or any copies thereof, that is in the possession of the Receiving Party will be considered for return to the Disclosing Party or for destruction, pending Receiving Party leadership approval.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty and weakens the obligation to return or destroy confidential information. The Receiving Party no longer has an automatic obligation to return or destroy, as the decision is now 'considered' and subject to internal approval. This change contradicts Section 3 'Information of the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party,' because the information is no longer automatically returned to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Information of the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party agrees to protect the Information of the Disclosing Party against unauthorized disclosure and warrants that it applies reasonable safeguards against the unauthorized disclosure Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated the return or destruction of confidential information. The changed text introduces an element of discretion ('considered for return... pending Receiving Party leadership approval') that did not previously exist. This directly contradicts the section stating 'Information of the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party' because the Receiving Party now has the power to delay or refuse returning the information, which means the information is no longer automatically and unconditionally remaining the Disclosing Party's property. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.
